meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Students: Build Passive Income to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to generate passive income while solving the no-portfolio problem. An advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for developers.
content.answer_block: Transform your lack of experience into a high-value asset by building a micro-SaaS that generates passive income. By leveraging AI-assisted development tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can ship a production-ready application, prove your technical mastery to employers, and build a portfolio that stands out immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the developer's greatest adversary. You have the skills, but your GitHub profile is a graveyard of tutorials, and recruiters see 'zero experience' where you see potential. The industry doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about what you've deployed.

We are going to flip the script. Instead of chasing entry-level roles, you will build a revenue-generating micro-SaaS. By focusing on passive income, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting a business, demonstrating product-market fit, and proving you can ship software that users actually pay for.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a small, painful task for a specific niche. Focus on high-intent, low-complexity problems.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a product strategist. Generate 5 micro-SaaS ideas that solve specific pain points for remote workers, focusing on automation and subscription potential.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Research top 100 Chrome extension categories with high recurring revenue potential.'

steps.title: Architecting with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold a robust, scalable architecture that handles authentication and payments out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate for a Next.js 14 application with Supabase auth, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS. Include a clean directory structure for API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Initialize project structure based on Antigravity best practices.'

steps.title: Rapid Feature Iteration
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on core functionality, ensuring code is production-grade and well-documented.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular service layer for handling Stripe webhooks and database persistence for user subscriptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement Stripe webhook handler with error logging and user tier updates.'

steps.title: Automated Testing & QA
steps.description: Build trust by implementing an automated test suite. Employers love seeing 'Testing' on a resume.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest unit tests for the core subscription logic and API middleware. Ensure 90% coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate comprehensive unit tests for all core business logic files.'

steps.title: Production Deployment & Monitoring
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel with observability. A portfolio piece that is live and monitored is worth 100 sandbox apps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a configuration guide for Vercel deployment with environment variables and Sentry integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Configure CI/CD pipeline and integrate error monitoring via Sentry.'

pro_tips: Never build a full-stack CRUD app; build a 'tool' that solves one specific problem for a paying user.
Document your failures in a 'Technical Journal' inside your repo; it shows maturity to senior engineers.
Prioritize Stripe integration early; nothing proves your capability like handling real money.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code into 'clean code' patterns before pushing to your public repo.
Market your tool on IndieHackers or Twitter while building; 'building in public' is the best resume.
content.next_steps: After launching, focus on acquiring your first 10 users. Use the feedback to iterate, then update your README with a 'case study' section detailing the architecture decisions you made.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a live product. Stop waiting for permission and start building your legacy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
